mission.title: crossplane: Support patching from common data sources?
mission.description: Support patching from common data sources?. Requested by 32+ users.

steps.title: Check current crossplane deployment
steps.description:
Verify your crossplane version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
helm list -n crossplane 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working crossplane installation.

steps.title: Review crossplane configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant crossplane configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
kubectl get configmap -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=crossplane
```
### References

Design: #3008
Implementation: #3007 

### What problem are you facing?

When platform teams design their XRs they may want to use environment scoped data to render out composed resources.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
kubectl get events -n crossplane --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support patching from common data sources?" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
### Description of your changes
Enables https://github.com/crossplane/crossplane/issues/2099#issuecomment-768301167

Current a fromFieldPath patch is a no-op if the specified 'from field path' does not actually exist. This commit allows such patches to be marked as 'Required', causing them to return an error if the field path they're patching from does not (yet) exist.
codeSnippets: Then `Composition` would look like the following:

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with crossplane installed or the issue environment reproducible.
